findings.message: writing-guidelines is a complete, valid skill (has its own SKILL.md matching sibling conventions) but is missing from skills.sh.json, so it won't surface in marketplace/skills.sh browsing.
Uploads a tarball of the project to an external deploy endpoint; this is the script's documented purpose and the tarball already excludes node_modules/.git/.env before upload.
Uploads a tarball of the project to an external deploy endpoint; this is the script's documented purpose and the tarball already excludes node_modules/.git/.env before upload.
3 rule files exist on disk but are not listed in SKILL.md's Quick Reference: design-system-compound-components.md, scroll-position-no-state.md, state-ground-truth.md.
Skill performs deploy/env/domain actions but has no explicit output/response-format section, unlike sibling deploy-to-vercel/SKILL.md which has a dedicated ## Output section.
Skill fetches its entire ruleset and output format from a mutable main branch ref on every invocation instead of a pinned commit or tag.
Skill fetches its entire ruleset and output format from a mutable main branch ref on every invocation instead of a pinned commit or tag.

Skill fetches its entire ruleset and output format from a mutable main branch ref on every invocation instead of a pinned commit or tag. — WebFetch target is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/web-interface-guidelines/main/command.md, ref is the mutable main branch, and the fetched content is used as both the rule set and the output-format instructions

Skill fetches its entire ruleset and output format from a mutable main branch ref on every invocation instead of a pinned commit or tag. — WebFetch target is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vercel-labs/writing-guidelines/main/command.md, ref is the mutable main branch, and the fetched content is used as both the rule set and the output-format instructions

Uploads a tarball of the project to an external deploy endpoint; this is the script's documented purpose and the tarball already excludes node_modules/.git/.env before upload. — curl -s -X POST "$DEPLOY_ENDPOINT" -F "file=@$TARBALL" -F "framework=$FRAMEWORK" uploads the packaged project to https://claude-skills-deploy.vercel.com/api/deploy

Uploads a tarball of the project to an external deploy endpoint; this is the script's documented purpose and the tarball already excludes node_modules/.git/.env before upload. — curl -s -X POST "$DEPLOY_ENDPOINT" -F "file=@$TARBALL" -F "framework=$FRAMEWORK" uploads the packaged project to https://codex-deploy-skills.vercel.sh/api/deploy
Skill performs deploy/env/domain actions but has no explicit output/response-format section, unlike sibling deploy-to-vercel/SKILL.md which has a dedicated ## Output section.

3 rule files exist on disk but are not listed in SKILL.md's Quick Reference: design-system-compound-components.md, scroll-position-no-state.md, state-ground-truth.md. — skills/react-native-skills/rules/ has 36 non-template files; SKILL.md Quick Reference table lists 33 rule names

writing-guidelines is a complete, valid skill (has its own SKILL.md matching sibling conventions) but is missing from skills.sh.json, so it won't surface in marketplace/skills.sh browsing. — skills.sh.json groupings list 8 skill names across React/Vercel/Design categories; skills/ contains 9 skill directories, and writing-guidelines is absent from every grouping
